questions: Where does the gate-artifact singleton overwrite occur and what does it destroy?
Which consumers read the unversioned <phase>.json path (compat surface)?
Can audit_history rows carry a versioned filename without a schema amendment?
How must provenance sidecars behave so verify_committed stays green?

findings.summary: write_artifact overwrites .qor/gates/<sid>/<phase>.json in place; re-run destroys prior bytes AND <phase>.provenance (live repro)
check_prior_artifact / read_phase_artifact / tier_guard resolve the singleton only
audit_history rows carry no filename; audit schema allows additionalProperties so artifact_filename is additive
gate_chain_completeness, verify_committed, evidence_bundle all join on singleton names; singleton must remain a byte-identical latest copy
atomic-write, QOR_SKILL_ACTIVE provenance binding, and hook fail-open invariants preserved; hook/sidecar should bind the versioned path
